title: ICU pain review documentation
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports pain severity, daily activity, sleep, participation, physical and psychological wellbeing, medicine response and adverse effects, coping, clinician-led treatment planning, early and regular review, and specialist referral without mapping medicine names or doses.
assessment.rationale: Supports entered pain assessment, impact, treatment context and review for ICU pain review; it does not generate a clinical decision.

source.title: Neuropathic pain in adults: pharmacological management in non-specialist settings
source.population: Adults with neuropathic pain managed in non-specialist settings.
source.setting: Non-specialist assessment, treatment review, and specialist referral.
source.exclusions: Exact for adult neuropathic-pain review and treatment monitoring; partial for a generic neuropathy workflow and not directly validated for UAE prescribing or referral pathways.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.7 — assessment, treatment planning, referral, and review

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports pain severity, daily activity, sleep, participation, physical and psychological wellbeing, medicine response and adverse effects, coping, clinician-led treatment planning, early and regular review, and specialist referral without mapping medicine names or doses.

assessment.text: Supports documenting mobility, previous or current pressure ulcer, nutritional status, cognition, sensory impairment, posture, deformity, skin integrity, colour change, heat, firmness, moisture, and pain only when assessed.
assessment.rationale: Supports documenting pressure-related pain when assessed for ICU pain review; it does not generate a clinical decision.

source.title: Pressure ulcers: prevention and management — Recommendations
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE adaptation
source.population: Adults, children, young people, infants, and neonates at risk of or with a pressure ulcer.
source.setting: Primary, community, emergency, secondary, and tertiary care pressure-risk and wound assessment.
source.exclusions: Exact for documenting pressure-ulcer risk factors, skin assessment, ulcer surface area, depth, undermining, category, photography, and repeated assessment. It does not generate a risk score, diagnosis, treatment, dressing, offloading, referral, or urgency.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.7 — adult risk and skin assessment

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting mobility, previous or current pressure ulcer, nutritional status, cognition, sensory impairment, posture, deformity, skin integrity, colour change, heat, firmness, moisture, and pain only when assessed.
